[[File:Agape feast 03.jpg|thumb|[[Fresco]] of a female figure holding a chalice at an early Christian [[Agape feast]]. [[Catacombs of Marcellinus and Peter|Catacomb]] of Saints [[Marcellinus and Peter]], [[Via Labicana]], [[Rome]].]]

Agape is the love of [[Omega]] it is an unconditional desire to make people's lives better for its own sake

It is covered a lot in [[Reacting to the Meaning Crisis 16]]<ref>https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5qK99VUDh-o</ref>. 

Agape refers to the love of creation and existential transformation. Agape is the love of parent for child. You don’t try and consume a child. An there is no reciprocity, no friendship. You love it because through your love you turn a non-person into a person.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Agape - Awakening from the Meaning Crisis|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210307151452/https://awakeningfromthemeaningcrisis.com/agape|website=web.archive.org|date=2021-03-07|access-date=2022-11-10}}</ref> This is why it is the love of Omega, because it helps bring something [[to Omega]].

Everyone depends on agape because people loved you before you were a person that you have become the person you are. Love turns non-person animals into moral agent persons.<ref name=":0" />

“It’s like, if I could just care about enough about my sofa , it would turn into a Ferrari or something.”<ref name=":0" />
==See Also==

* [[Xenia]]
* [[Storge]]

==Further reading==

*https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5qK99VUDh-o

==References==
[[Category:John Vervaeke]]
[[sn:Agape]][[Category:Love]]
[[sn:Agape]][[Category:Agape]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
[[sn:Agape]][[sn:Agape]]<references />
[[Category:Omega]][[Category:Oldest pages before the refresh]]
[[Category:Pages]]
